steps.title: Define the support needed
steps.content: The experts' claim is that high-speed rail is more cost-effective than highway systems. A supporting finding should compare what each system costs with what each system delivers, such as how much traffic capacity each option provides for the money spent.

steps.title: Reject side issues
steps.content: Choice B focuses on old rail infrastructure, which could make rail harder or more expensive to build. Choice C mentions a long-term maintenance fund, not whether rail gives more value than highways. Choice D discusses travel preferences in Turkey and Germany rather than cost-effectiveness in the United States. Choice A directly says highway capacity is more expensive relative to accommodated traffic than high-speed rail capacity is. The answer is A.
